hero.subtitle: Websites, web apps, IT stuff, and network headaches. Full-stack developer and field technician based in Otumba, Estado de Mexico. I also speak English, if that helps.

services.title: What can i build for you
services.subtitle-one: I'm not just a developer, i've done everything from fiber optic cables to building full-stack apps deployed on the cloud. Here's what i can actually help you with.
services.subtitle-two: I keep my queue to 3 projects so i can actually focus on yours, no assembly line stuff.

one.title: Full-stack web application
one.description: Need a complete app? User logins, an admin panel, orders, whatever? I'll build it backend and frontend. Angular + ASP.NET, deployed and everything. No templates, built for you specifically.

two.title: Frontend-only website
two.description: Landing page, portfolio, business site. No backend needed. Looks great, loads fast, works on mobile. Done in about a week.

three.title: Network & IT
three.description: Fiber optic termination, UTP crimping, router config, troubleshooting? PC upgrades, updates, or custom rig builds? I've done ISP-level field work, no problem is too deep in the weeds.

four.title: Desktop application (Windows)
four.description: Windows app that needs to work offline, talk to hardware, or do something a website can't? I've got you. I work with WPF/.NET, MVVM architecture, and i enjoy solving weird, specific problems.

five.title: Bilingual translation / localization (EN/ES)
five.description: Spanish ↔ English. Your website, app strings, documents, or emails. Native Spanish, fluent English. I can translate documents, websites, or act as a bilingual point of contact. Technical and general content welcome.

six.title: Maintenance & fixes
six.description: Got a site that's broken, slow, or outdated? Need a new feature for a pre-existing website? Send it my way. One-off fixes or ongoing maintenance. I don't ghost clients after delivery.

portfolio.title: Stuff i've shipped
portfolio.subtitle: Real projects, real clients, real deployments. None of these are tutorial projects or 'practice apps.'

atelier.title: Atelier Geode
atelier.description: A custom web platform for a bespoke picture framing artisan. Angular 19 frontend, ASP.NET Core API, PostgreSQL database, Azure Blob Storage, JWT auth, Gmail + Resend email function, admin dashboard, real-time price configurator, bilingual support, Docker deployment. Deployed and live.

orchestra.title: OrchestraMC
orchestra.description: WPF Windows desktop app that deploys a Minecraft server and handles tunneling so anyone can connect. Handles the Forge installation, Ngrok/Playit.gg proxies management, has a server.properties editor, simple one-click startup. No port forwarding, no tech knowledge required. Integrates Ngrok + Playit.gg.

methods.title: What you see is what you get
methods.subtitle: I don't hide my tools or oversell my range. This is my exact stack, no surprises mid-project. If your project fits this, we'll work great together. If it doesn't, I'll tell you upfront.

angular.title: Angular
angular.subtitle: v17 to v20 preferred · AngularJS possible but not recommended

description.one: My primary frontend framework. I write modern Angular, so signals, standalone components, the new control flow syntax
description.two: I specifically avoid the legacy NgModule + RxJS-everywhere style if I can help it, because the modern approach is far cleaner.  I can work with older Angular projects, but if you're starting fresh, we're going v17 minimum.

react.title: React
react.pill: Can Do · Not my first choice
react.subtitle: Hooks, functional components, standard patterns
react.description: I can build in React and the code will be solid. But Angular is where I'm fastest and most comfortable, so if you give me a choice, I'll pick Angular every time. If your project is already in React, no problem, i'll work with it.

aspnet.title: ASP.NET Core
aspnet.subtitle: Web API · EF Core · JWT Auth · .NET 8/9
aspnet.description: My backend of choice. REST APIs, JWT auth, role-based authorization, Entity Framework Core for the ORM layer. I structure with proper service/repository separation, not just controllers doing everything.

postgre.title: PostgreSQL
postgre.subtitle: Primary DB · EF Core migrations · raw SQL when needed
postgre.description: My go-to database. I use EF Core migrations for schema management, write raw queries when performance demands it, and I know how to handle things like UTC timezone awareness properly. Learned that one the hard way. '>_>

description.one: I build Windows desktop apps in WPF with MVVM architecture. Proper ViewModel/View separation, data binding, command patterns, service injection. WinForms is where I started and I can still read and maintain it, but I won't start new projects in it. WPF just produces better-looking apps.
description.two: I started on .NET Framework 4.2 and know the full migration path to modern .NET Core. I did this mid-development on OrchestraMC: swapping
description.three: fixing async patterns, updating strings to their nullable counterparts -- the works. If you have a legacy desktop app that needs modernising, that's actually something I've done under pressure before.

css.title: On CSS: Vanilla BEM. No Tailwind, no SCSS.
css.subtitle: I write plain CSS with BEM naming convention. No utility-class frameworks, no preprocessors.

advantages.title: Why BEM
list: Zero build-step dependency since it's just CSS
Self-documenting class names you can read months later
No framework to migrate away from when it goes out of fashion

disadvantages.title: The Tradeoff
list: No built-in design token system (I add CSS variables manually)
Tailwind prototypes faster for throwaway UIs

css.note: If your existing codebase uses Tailwind, I can work with it, but for greenfield projects, I'll default to this.

one.title: Mobile apps (iOS/Android)
one.description: Native mobile is a whole different world. I'm not your person for that.

two.title: Vue, Svelte, other frameworks
two.description: They're easy so i can probably figure it out, but I won't take your money to learn on the job.

three.title: Python / Node / PHP backends
three.description: My backend is .NET. I don't do Laravel, Django, or Express.

four.title: Rush jobs under 3 days
four.description: Good work takes time. I won't cut corners to meet an unrealistic deadline.

demos.title: "Can you add X feature?" Probably yes. Try it!
demos.subtitle: These are demos of real features from projects I've built. Click "Give It A Try!" and try one of those plonkers on to peek under the hood.

options.label: Admin Dashboard: Manage your content, no developer needed.
Dynamic Price Configurator: live total as customers choose options
Image Gallery: cloud-hosted, expandable, no storage limits
Secure Login: JWT tokens, role-based access, session management
Language Toggle: ngx-translate runtime, no page reload
Email Integration: MailKit and Resend for professional emails

demos.idle: Click a feature to see a live preview!

auth.bottom-text: Your admin panel is behind a JWT-authenticated wall. Nobody gets in without the password.

i18n.bottom-text: ✓ ngx-translate · Zero page reload · Persists across routes

email.title: ✅ Your commission has been confirmed!
email.text: Hi there! Your order has been received and is now in the queue. You'll hear from us within 2 business days. Order #
email.bottom-text: ✓ Sent via MailKit · Custom templates · Automatic triggers

it.title: I also show up with a screwdriver
it.subtitle: Not everything is a website. Sometimes you need someone who can pull cable, fix a printer, or upgrade a computer that's been running on a spinning HDD since 2011. I cover a reasonable radius around Otumba de Gómez Farías. If you're outside the circle... we can still talk remotely.

one.title: Maintenance
one.content: Printhead cleaning, nozzle alignment, ink system flushing. The stuff you're supposed to do every six months but nobody does.

two.title: Wireless Setup
two.content: Getting a printer to actually stay on the network, work with multiple devices, and not vanish every time the router reboots.

three.title: Fresh Installation
three.content: New printer, right drivers, working on every PC in the office. Including the one running Windows 7 in the corner.

one.title: Static IP / DHCP lockdown
one.content: Assign fixed IPs to every device so only known nodes get internet access and strangers can't just connect and steal your bandwidth. MAC filtering, access control.

two.title: Router Configuration
two.content: TP-Link routers, Ubiquiti POEs, double-bandwidth; I've configured all of these. Fresh install, port forwarding, VLANs, bandwidth limits per device, firmware updates.

three.title: Full ISP-style Deployment
three.content: End-to-end provisioning from fiber termination to client-side router config.

cabling.title: Structured Cabling
cabling.subtitle: UTP · RJ45 · fiber optic · Cat6 SFTP

one.title: UTP Crimping & Testing
one.content: Crimping RJ45s, continuity testing, fixing broken cable runs. I work with whatever cable you already have installed.

two.title: Fiber Optic Termination
two.content: Fusion splicing two fiber cables, SC connector termination, signal loss testing with an OTDR. The works. Professional ISP-grade experience, this is what I did in the field at Offilex.

three.title: I bring my own cable if you need it
three.content: I carry a 100m spool of Cat6 double-shielded SFTP with metal RJ45 connectors. This is serious cabling, shielded against interference, suitable for demanding environments. If you need industrial-level cable, I got it.

one.title: HDD to SSD Upgrades
one.content: The single most impactful thing you can do to an old computer. Clean Windows 11 install on a bootable USB. Night and day difference.

two.title: Custom PC Builds
two.content: From bare motherboard to finished machine; CPU, thermal paste, RAM, cable management, BIOS config. Built my own rig from scratch (DDR5 and everything).

three.title: Diagnosis & Repair
three.content: Slow machine, overheating, won't boot; I'll figure out what's wrong before recommending you spend money on anything.

it.note: All in-person work is subject to my coverage zone, see the map below. Remote diagnosis and consulting is available anywhere.
